claim_text: Zimbabwe's 2000–2008 land-reform-plus-monetisation episode produced hyperinflation because the central bank monetised fiscal deficits once property-right expropriation collapsed the tax base — an instance of fiscal dominance.
hypothesis_claim: Every documented hyperinflation episode since 1900 (Weimar Germany, post-WW2 Hungary, Yugoslavia 1990s, Zimbabwe 2000s, Venezuela 2010s-2020s, among others) was preceded by fiscal dominance — a state of affairs where monetary policy is subordinated to financing government deficits that cannot be financed by taxation or market-rate borrowing. Monetary mismanagement alone, absent fiscal dominance, has not produced hyperinflation in the historical record.

covers_claims_entry.notes: Austrian claim that Zimbabwe 2000-2008 land-reform-plus-monetisation exemplifies fiscal dominance pathway; aligned with hypothesis's Zimbabwe episode.

error_messages: treatment_tags: claim treatment_tags ['zimbabwe_hyperinflation_2008', 'zimbabwe_land_reform_2000'] disjoint from hypothesis treatment_tags ['fiscal_dominance', 'hyperinflation_episodes']

claim_text: Milei's 2023–2024 fiscal-first shock therapy (eliminating primary deficit, ending central bank deficit monetisation) will show faster inflation stabilisation than any prior Argentine programme that tried price controls or heterodox stabilisation first.
claim_notes: Austrian prediction: removing the fiscal-dominance root produces inflation decline within 12-24 months. Audit flagged as FAIL — claim scope is Milei 2023-2024 disinflation but current hypothesis (argentina_default_collapse_output_effects) tests Menem 1991-2001 convertibility collapse. Phase 4C: candidate for relink to milei_reforms_reduce_argentine_inflation (better era/entity match) or new hypothesis milei_fiscal_first_disinflation_vs_heterodox_predecessors.
hypothesis_claim: Argentine convertibility and subsequent collapse (Menem 1991–2001) reflects rule-based money's appeal combined with fixed-regime rigidity risk that Austrian theory predicts.

covers_claims_entry.notes: Milei 2023-2024 fiscal-first shock therapy claim; Phase 4C concern: claim scope is Milei 2023-2024 but hypothesis tests Menem 1991-2001 convertibility collapse — candidate for relink to milei_reforms_reduce_argentine_inflation or new milei_fiscal_first_disinflation_vs_heterodox_predecessors hypothesis.

error_messages: period: claim period [2023, 2025] disjoint from hypothesis period [1991, 2005]
treatment_tags: claim treatment_tags ['fiscal_dominance_removal', 'milei_shock_therapy_2023'] disjoint from hypothesis treatment_tags ['argentine_crisis_2001', 'menem_convertibility_1991']

claim_text: Pre-1914 classical-gold-standard episodes (excluding wartime suspensions) show lower long-run average inflation than any comparable-length fiat-regime sample, even if short-run price-level volatility is higher.
claim_notes: Austrian steelman: long-run purchasing-power stability matters more than short-run volatility. Audit flagged as FAIL — claim scope is pre-1914 classical-gold-standard era but current hypothesis (fiat_expansion_erodes_currency_purchasing_power_long_run) tests post-1971 fiat era. Phase 4C: candidate for new hypothesis classical_gold_standard_vs_fiat_long_run_inflation_comparison or claim rewrite to post-1971 framing.
hypothesis_claim: Over 50+ year horizons since the 1971 collapse of Bretton Woods, major fiat currencies (USD, GBP, EUR legacy components, JPY, AUD) have lost substantial purchasing power against hard assets (gold, broad commodity baskets, residential real estate). This is documented fact; the hypothesis re-establishes the pattern using provenanced public data.

covers_claims_entry.notes: Austrian pre-1914 classical-gold-standard comparison claim; Phase 4C concern: claim scope is pre-1914 gold era but hypothesis tests post-1971 fiat era — candidate for new hypothesis classical_gold_standard_vs_fiat_long_run_inflation_comparison or claim rewrite to post-1971 framing.

error_messages: period: claim period [1870, 1914] disjoint from hypothesis period [1971, 2025]
treatment_tags: claim treatment_tags ['classical_gold_standard_1870'] disjoint from hypothesis treatment_tags ['bretton_woods_collapse_1971', 'fiat_regime']
